tool_ts.usage: DEAD IMPORT — ToolCache is never referenced anywhere in tool.ts. The `cacheable` field on `Def` interface (line 104) is a plain property, not wired to ToolCache.

usages.purpose: Only actual call — never calls getOrCompute

registry_ts.verdict: Imports ToolCache but only uses cache.stats(). getOrCompute is never called. cacheable flag is passed through in tools() output (line 528) but consumed only by session/tools.ts for semaphore selection.

app_runtime_ts.verdict: Only provides the layer. No method calls.

session_tools_ts.usage: cacheable flag is read from item at line 89: `const toolSemaphore = (item as any).cacheable ? readSemaphore : writeSemaphore`
session_tools_ts.verdict: cacheable flag used for semaphore routing, NOT for ToolCache routing. The caching infrastructure is completely bypassed.

refs_created_in_layer.name: cache
refs_created_in_layer.purpose: Stores cached tool results with expiration

refs_created_in_layer.name: inflight
refs_created_in_layer.purpose: Deduplicates concurrent compute for same key

key_data_mutations.cache_map_direct_mutation: cache.ts:82-84 — map.delete(key), map.set(key, entry) on Map ref from Ref.get, NOT via Ref.update. Relies on Map reference identity.
key_data_mutations.inflight_direct_mutation: cache.ts:108-116 — pending = yield* Ref.get(inflight); ... pending.set(key, d). Direct mutation, not Ref.update.
key_data_mutations.ensuring_cleanup: cache.ts:119-123 — Ref.update(inflight, (m) => { m.delete(key); return m }). Uses Ref.update but still mutates-in-place and returns same ref.
key_data_mutations.cache_insertion: cache.ts:198-208 — Ref.update(cache, (m) => { ... m.set(key, ...); return m }). Same in-place mutation pattern.

section_3_internal_state_analysis.dedup_mechanism: Uses mutable Map obtained via Ref.get. Direct mutation (pending.set) makes inflight entry visible to other fibers before the compute starts. Since all fibers share the same Map reference, subsequent Ref.get calls see the added Deferred.

race_1_inflight_cache_gap.window: Between inflight cleanup (ensuring at line 119-123) and cache insertion (Ref.update at line 198-208)
race_1_inflight_cache_gap.description: After compute() finishes with success, the Effect.ensuring callback removes the key from the inflight map BEFORE the cache is populated. A concurrent fiber arriving in this gap sees: cache miss (not yet inserted) → inflight miss (already cleaned up) → starts a DUPLICATE compute.
timeline: t0: Fiber A compute() finishes, Exit captured
t1: ensuring() runs — inflight map deletes key
t2: Fiber B getOrCompute — Ref.get(cache) → MISS
t3: Fiber B getOrCompute — Ref.get(inflight) → MISS (cleaned at t1!)
t4: Fiber B creates new Deferred, starts DUPLICATE compute
t5: Fiber A Deferred.done() — resolves original deferred
t6: Fiber A Ref.update(cache) — inserts result into cache
race_1_inflight_cache_gap.result: Two concurrent computations run for the same key. Wasted work. Potentially stale results if compute has side effects.
race_1_inflight_cache_gap.fix: Swap order: populate cache BEFORE clearing inflight. Or use a single Ref with a tagged union (Cached | Computing | Inflight).

race_2_direct_map_mutation.window: Bypassing Ref.update for the inflight map
race_2_direct_map_mutation.description: The inflight map entry is added via direct mutation (pending.set at line 116) on a reference obtained from Ref.get. This works because Map is a reference type and Ref.get returns the same reference. However: (a) it breaks Ref's immutability semantics, (b) if Ref ever returned a copy, dedup silently breaks, (c) the ensuring cleanup uses the inconsistent Ref.update pattern.
race_2_direct_map_mutation.result: Fragile synchronization. Works by accident of JS reference semantics.

race_3_invalidateKey_does_not_clear_infl.description: invalidateKey (line 158) deletes from the cache map but does not touch the inflight map. A compute already in flight continues and caches its result AFTER invalidation. Invalidation is effectively lost for in-flight computations.
race_3_invalidateKey_does_not_clear_infl.result: Stale cached result if the intent was to prevent the result from being cached.

section_5_test_surface.dedicated_tests: NONE — No test file exists for cache.ts
section_5_test_surface.indirect_coverage: NONE — ToolCache (getOrCompute) is never called from any production or test code
section_5_test_surface.cacheStats_calls: registry.cacheStats() is defined but never called from any consumer
relevant_test_files_checked: packages/opencode/test/session/llm.test.ts — test token cache only, not ToolCache
section_5_test_surface.verdict: Zero test coverage. 221 lines of untested concurrent logic with known race conditions.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: ToolCache inflight dedup has a TOCTOU gap allowing duplicate compute calls
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: The ensuring inflight cleanup at lines 119-123 runs BEFORE cache insertion at lines 198-208. A concurrent fiber can observe (cache=miss, inflight=miss) and start a second compute for the same key.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: ToolCache is defined but 100% dead code — getOrCompute never called
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: getOrCompute exists with full LRU eviction, TTL, max entry size, inflight dedup, stats. No caller ever invokes it. 8 tools declare cacheable:true but the flag only controls semaphore selection in session/tools.ts, not caching.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: Dead import in tool.ts — ToolCache imported but never referenced
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: import * as ToolCache from './cache' is imported but never used in the file beyond the import statement.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: Inflight map mutated via direct reference instead of Ref.update
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: pending.set(key, d) directly mutates the Map stored in Ref. Breaks Ref abstraction. Works only because JS Map is a reference type.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: Zero tests for concurrent caching logic
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: 221-line module with Ref-based concurrent state, LRU eviction, TTL expiration, and inflight dedup has zero tests. Race condition SG-1 would be caught by a simple concurrent test.

section_6_smoking_guns.summary: invalidateKey does not clear in-flight computations
section_6_smoking_guns.detail: invalidateKey only deletes from the cache map. If a compute for that key is in-flight, it completes and caches its result, effectively ignoring the invalidation.
